description:
Receive's cont-frame alloc uses a typer-resolved SpecId, mirroring .29.12.1's pattern.

## What

In `emit_receive` (`src/ir_codegen.rs:3009-3044`), the cont frame is allocated for `cont.fn_id`'s any-key today. Replace with resolved SpecId:
- Build key as `[Descr::any(), ...captured_descrs]` (slot 0 = any per the typer's `Term::Receive` handling at `src/ir_typer.rs:214`).
- `resolved_sid = spec_registry.resolve(cont.fn_id, &key).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...))`.
- `fz_alloc_frame(resolved_sid, schemas[resolved_sid].size)`.

## Why

Receive's continuation is opaque on the result side (mailbox value type isn't statically known per recv), but the captured-vars side IS specialized. The cont's narrow spec — keyed by `[any, captured_descrs...]` — should be the dispatch target. Same pattern as .29.12.1 for direct calls.

## Tests (TDD)

- A receive inside a fn with typed captures resolves to a cont SpecId whose body is the typer's narrow specialization.
- Existing receive tests stay green.
- Three-path parity.

## Note

This may overlap with .29.12.1 if that ticket's helper already covers Term::Receive. If so, this ticket reduces to verification + integration test.
title: Receive ABI uses resolved SpecId for cont frame
